response:
MOORE'S LAW IS PREDICTED TO EVENTUALLY CEASE TO HOLD AS PHYSICAL LIMITATIONS IN SEMICONDUCTOR MANUFACTURING BECOME MORE PROMINENT. THIS OCCURS BECAUSE AS TRANSISTORS GET SMALLER, THEY APPROACH THE ATOMIC SCALE, MAKING FURTHER MINIATURIZATION INFEASIBLE WITH CURRENT MATERIALS AND TECHNIQUES. EXPERTS ESTIMATE THAT THIS LIMIT MAY BE REACHED WITHIN THE NEXT FEW DECADES, DEPENDING ON ADVANCEMENTS IN ALTERNATIVE TECHNOLOGIES SUCH AS QUANTUM COMPUTING OR 3D INTEGRATION.

***  
THE TRANSITION FROM MOORE'S LAW WILL NOT BE A SUDDEN STOP BUT A GRADUAL SHIFT TOWARD NEW PARADIGMS IN COMPUTING. INSTEAD OF FOCUSING SOLELY ON INCREASING TRANSISTOR DENSITY, THE INDUSTRY MAY PRIORITIZE IMPROVING EFFICIENCY, SPECIALIZED HARDWARE, AND NEW ARCHITECTURES. THIS EVOLUTION WILL REQUIRE INNOVATION IN BOTH HARDWARE AND SOFTWARE, BUT IT WILL MARK THE END OF THE ERA WHERE PROCESSOR PERFORMANCE DOUBLES EVERY TWO YEARS.  
STOP
